question: Will the ceasefire between the U.S. and Yemen’s Houthis mediated by Oman be broken before June 2026?
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as **Yes** if either the U.S. military or Houthi forces engage in direct attacks against the other between after May 6, 2025 and before June 1, 2026, according to [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). The strike must be targeted against the U.S. or Houthis. Accidental strikes or collateral losses will not resolve this question.
background: Following a nearly-two month bombing campaign by the US, Oman announced in May 2025 that it had successfully mediated a [ceasefire agreement](https://www.reuters.com/world/trump-says-us-will-stop-bombing-houthis-after-agreement-struck-2025-05-06/) between the US and Yemen's Houthis group. The ceasefire was intended to halt direct hostilities while opening the door to longer-term negotiations, though the precise terms and duration of the agreement have yet to be announced.
